intro: TelemetryDeck is freemium; App Radar is paid subscription. TelemetryDeck: Privacy-focused mobile app analytics with GDPR-compliant signal tracking and RevenueCat integration for subscription metrics. App Radar: ASO and App Store management platform with keyword tracking, metadata editor, review management, and competitor analysis. TelemetryDeck fits indie Apple-platform developers wanting privacy-friendly product analytics. App Radar fits mobile — in practice, mobile apps with subscriptions. On our rubric TelemetryDeck scores 4/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency, while App Radar scores 3/5 and 3/5. The honest trade-off: TelemetryDeck's main drawback — Not a full product analytics platform; App Radar's — Free apps without monetization. One point in TelemetryDeck's favour: Privacy-first, GDPR-friendly signal tracking. TelemetryDeck is explicitly not the right pick for cross-platform apps needing deep funnels and behavioral analytics. App Radar is not aimed at free apps without monetization. Another TelemetryDeck advantage: Affordable vs Mixpanel and Amplitude. Another App Radar advantage: Paywall A/B testing. Another TelemetryDeck friction point: Limited cross-platform coverage vs competitors.
quick_take: TelemetryDeck is for indie Apple-platform developers wanting privacy-friendly product analytics; App Radar is for mobile; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose TelemetryDeck if indie Apple-platform developers wanting privacy-friendly product analytics, and if a free tier with an optional paid path matters.
verdict_b: Choose App Radar if your work looks like mobile apps with subscriptions, and if you accept tighter coupling in exchange for the managed surface.

faq.q: Is TelemetryDeck cheaper than App Radar?
faq.a: TelemetryDeck uses freemium pricing; App Radar uses paid subscription. Which is cheaper depends on your volume and team size — model both against your projected usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from TelemetryDeck to App Radar?
faq.a: Migrating out of TelemetryDeck is straightforward — it's rated low lock-in (Privacy-first, signals exportable). The harder question is how deeply you commit to App Radar afterward, since it sits at medium lock-in (ASO data exportable). Plan the forward cost, not just the exit.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, TelemetryDeck or App Radar?
faq.a: TelemetryDeck scores higher in our rubric (4/5 vs 3/5 for App Radar). Transparency is 5/5 for TelemetryDeck and 3/5 for App Radar. DX scores are rubric-based, not benchmarks, so evaluate against your own toolchain.

faq.q: Is App Radar a good alternative to TelemetryDeck?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — App Radar is a plausible alternative for many TelemetryDeck use cases. It fits best when mobile. Skip it if free apps without monetization.
